content:
# 🚨 MANDATORY: Team Memory System 🚨

A shared team memory stores critical gotchas, conventions, and debugging insights across sessions.

## REQUIRED: /recall BEFORE Starting Any Task

You MUST run /recall at the start of every task to check for relevant prior knowledge. Skipping this risks repeating known mistakes and wasting time on already-solved problems.

```
/recall "<topic or keyword relevant to your task>"
```

Examples:
- Working on Stripe integration? → /recall "Stripe"
- Fixing a database query? → /recall "database" or /recall "Drizzle"
- Debugging a test? → /recall "testing patterns"
- Working on team creation? → /recall "team" or /recall "createId"
- Touching waivers? → /recall "waiver"

**Do NOT skip this step.** Past sessions have stored critical gotchas that will save significant debugging time.

## REQUIRED: /remember After Discoveries

When you discover something useful — a gotcha, root cause, convention, or debugging insight — you MUST save it immediately with /remember. Do not wait until the end of the session.

```
/remember "<what you learned>" --category=gotcha|convention|debugging|architecture|workflow --priority=critical|moderate|ephemeral
```

## Summary

- **BEFORE work**: /recall to load relevant memories (MANDATORY)
- **DURING work**: /remember as soon as you discover something worth saving (MANDATORY)
- Failure to use team memory means the team loses knowledge and repeats mistakes.

message.content:
Root cause confirmed — it's a data-model bug, not just one submission path

  The issue is structural and affects any view that reads scores by userId.

  The broken invariant

  scoresTable has a unique index on just (competitionEventId, userId) — apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/scores.ts:122-125. A single user can only have one score row per competition event,
  regardless of division. When an online competition reuses the same trackWorkout across both an individual and a partner division, a user registered in both divisions has one shared row. Every
  write is onDuplicateKeyUpdate, so the last submission wins and overwrites scalingLevelId.

  Where the bug manifests

  All read paths key scores by (competitionEventId, userId) with no division filter, so whichever row exists is attributed to every registration the user has:

  - Preview leaderboard — apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts:740-744 fetches by userIds, then at lines 882-893 maps each score back via filteredRations.find(r =>
  r.user.id === score.userId) and buckets into registration.registration.divisionId (NOT score.scalingLevelId). When the leaderboard is filtered to the individual division, it finds the individual
  registration and the partner-division score lands there.
  - Organizer athlete detail page (…/athletes/creg_…) — apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts:366-374 queries inArray(userId, …) AND inArray(competitionEventId, …) with no
  division filter.
  - getAthleteEventScoreFn — athlete-score-fns.ts:562-568 matches (competitionEventId, userId) only.

  Contributing bugs in write paths

  - athlete-score-fns.ts:359-375 (submitAthleteScoreFn) does registration lookup with .limit(1) and no divisionId filter — whichever registration returns first wins. The input schema also doesn't
  accept a divisionId (line 85-92). This file looks orphaned (not referenced anywhere), but still live code.
  - video-submission-fns.ts:1113-1147 and routes/api/compete/scores/submit.ts:2ectly scope the registration lookup by divisionId, but the upsert still collides on (competitionEventId,
  userId), so the row still gets clobbered.

  Recommended fix (not implemented yet — want your sign-off)

  1. Change the unique index to (competitionEventId, userId, scalingLevelId) so one user can hold a distinct row per division. This is the root fix.
  2. Read paths: filter scores by scalingLevelId = registration.divisionId in competition-leaderboard.ts (fetchScores + the grouping at line 890 should use score.scalingLevelId as ground truth),
  organizer-athlete-fns.ts, and getAthleteEventScoreFn.
  3. Write paths: require divisionId in submitAthleteScoreFn and /api/compete/scores/submit input, scope the registration lookup by it, and use onDuplicateKeyUpdate that matches the new 3-column
  unique key.
  4. Data backfill: existing score rows already have scalingLevelId, but we need to confirm no data will collide under the new index (it can't — only one row existed before).

  Tradeoff: This needmigration (pnpm db:generate) and touches several read/write sites. The alternative — adding an explicit registrationId FK to scoresTable — is cleaner but a bigger change.

  Want me to proceed with option 1 (unique-index fix + read/write updates) on this branch? --- please understand this issue. Given this unique constraint issue that we just uncovered we need to find what users are affected so essentially for this competition that I'm going to give you we have Many divisions. A lot of them are indie, which means there's only one person, and then there's partner divisions that have two people and you can sign up for as many divisions as you want. We didn't put any constraints on what divisions or how many divisions you can sign up. up. So we need to look for a couple cases in production. The first one being a captain who signed up for a partner division, say a male male partner rx division also signs up for a male rx individual division And the competition in question has four workouts where two of the partner workouts are unique to the partner division and two of the workouts number three and number four are shared with the individual division. So the issue is on three and four with in this scenario where that person who signs up for partner and individual when they try to submit their individual that will overwrite their partner division score or vice versa and then the other issue is say you're an individual athlete and you're not not quite sure what level you want to compete at, so you sign up for an RX and then an intermediate division. All four workouts are going to be shared there. So that's going to be a big issue, even though from a use case standpoint I expect that to be less frequent because most people will stick to a division. A non-issue that you might find is when an individual signs up for an RX division and then an individual RX Masters division. which if they're both if the masters division and the individual Rx division division or both RX, then the scores would be the same anyway. So the overwrite doesn't matter, but when we apply a new fix, we will need to duplicate that score. to whichever division doesn't get it

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_session_create: Create one or more child Devin sessions (private mode only)
- devin_session_interact: Manage a Devin session — get status, send messages, sleep/terminate/archive, read messages & attachments, manage tags (private mode only)
- devin_session_events: Inspect session events — list summaries, fetch full details, or search event contents (private mode only)
- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)
- list_integrations: List all native integrations and MCP servers with their status and settings URLs (private mode only)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- agent-browser: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction.
- seo-aeo-best-practices: SEO and AEO best practices for metadata, Open Graph, sitemaps, robots.txt, hreflang, JSON-LD structured data, EEAT, and content optimized for search engines and AI answer surfaces. Use this skill when implementing page SEO, technical SEO, schema markup, international SEO, AI-overview readiness, or improving content for Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and similar assistants.
- self-improving-agent: A universal self-improving agent that learns from ALL skill experiences. Uses multi-memory architecture (semantic + episodic + working) to continuously evolve the codebase. Auto-triggers on skill completion/error with hooks-based self-correction.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- linear: Manage issues, projects & team workflows in Linear. Use when the user wants to read, create or updates tickets in Linear.
- breadboarding: Transform a workflow description into affordance tables showing UI and Code affordances with their wiring. Use to map existing systems or design new ones from shaped parts.
- repo-autopsy: Clone a GitHub repo into a local cache and perform deep analysis using full local toolchain (rg, ast-grep, git, fd, tokei, gitleaks). Use when the user wants to inspect, explore, audit, or understand an external GitHub repository — structure, dependencies, hotspots, exports, secrets, code stats, git blame, or targeted file reads. Triggers on phrases like "autopsy", "analyze this repo", "clone and explore", "what's inside owner/repo", or any request to investigate a third-party GitHub project without adding it to the current working tree.
- adr-skill: Create and maintain Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) optimized for agentic coding workflows. Use when you need to propose, write, update, accept/reject, deprecate, or supersede an ADR; bootstrap an adr folder and index; consult existing ADRs before implementing changes; or enforce ADR conventions. This skill uses Socratic questioning to capture intent before drafting, and validates output against an agent-readiness checklist.
- breadboard-reflection: Breadboard Analysis
- stripe-best-practices: Best practices for building Stripe integrations
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- vercel-composition-patterns: React composition patterns that scale. Use when refactoring components with boolean prop proliferation, building flexible component libraries, or designing reusable APIs. Triggers on tasks involving compound components, render props, context providers, or component architecture. Includes React 19 API changes.
- shaping: Use this methodology when collaboratively shaping a solution with the user - iterating on problem definition (requirements) and solution options (shapes).
- tanstack-start-server-only: TanStack Start server-only patterns using createServerOnlyFn and cloudflare:workers env access.
Use when: accessing Cloudflare environment variables (APP_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, etc.),
creating server-only utility functions, working with Stripe or other server-only APIs,
or when you need to ensure code only runs on the server and throws if called from client.
Covers createServerOnlyFn vs createServerFn differences, env access patterns, and centralized
server-only utilities.
- documentation: Centralizes Diataxis documentation framework guidance so Claude can write, review, or improve documentation following the four distinct types (tutorials, how-to guides, reference, explanation).
- athlete-docs: Documentation guidance for competition athletes and volunteers in WODsmith. Use when writing, reviewing, or improving athlete-facing documentation including registration, scheduling, workout viewing, leaderboards, check-in, and volunteer coordination.
- ralph-mode: Autonomous AI coding loop that ships features while you sleep. Use when the user wants to run Ralph, start an autonomous coding session, work overnight, or let Claude implement multiple user stories autonomously. Triggers on "ralph", "autonomous mode", "overnight coding", "autonomous loop", or requests to implement multiple stories without supervision.
- server-fn-auth: Authentication and authorization patterns for TanStack Start server functions.
Use when: adding auth to a new server function, fixing missing authorization,
checking user permissions, protecting endpoints, or implementing organizer/volunteer
access control for competitions.
- test: Primary entry point for testing guidance. Routes to appropriate test type (unit, integration, E2E) based on what you're testing. Use when asked about "test", "testing", "write tests", "TDD", or test strategy. Embeds Testing Trophy philosophy and TDD Red-Green-Refactor as core discipline.
- claim-url-lookup: Look up claim URLs for placeholder users created via manual registration. Use when needing to find the sign-up claim link for a specific email address, debug claim token issues, or verify claim tokens exist in local KV. Triggers on "claim URL", "claim token", "claim link", "placeholder user sign-up link", or requests to find the registration invite link for an email.
- competition-organizer-docs: Generate and review documentation for competition organizers using Diataxis framework. Use when writing docs for organizer workflows like creating competitions, managing divisions, scheduling heats, handling registrations, entering results, or managing volunteers/judges.
- type-safety: Fix type assertions and improve TypeScript type safety. Use when encountering 'as unknown as' casts, manual type definitions that duplicate schema types, or unclear type errors in database queries, especially with Drizzle ORM relations. Also use when verifying types
- date-timezone: Handle date and timezone conversions correctly in forms and displays. Use when working with: Date inputs, new Date(), formatDate, toLocaleDateString, getDate(), getMonth(), getFullYear(), date parsing, UTC conversion, timezone issues, dates displaying wrong day, date-only fields in forms, YYYY-MM-DD parsing. Triggers: dates off by one day, timezone bugs, form date handling, competition dates, event dates, calendar dates.
- integration-test: Guide for writing integration tests with Vitest and Testing Library. Use when testing multi-component workflows, database interactions, React components with context providers, or full user flows. Covers the Testing Trophy philosophy (integration > unit), factory patterns for test data, MSW for network mocking, async testing patterns (waitFor, findBy), and custom render with providers. Use this for tests that cross multiple modules or layers of the application.
- permissions: Guide for working with team-based permissions and authorization in the WODsmith codebase. Use when touching TEAM_PERMISSIONS constants, hasTeamPermission/requireTeamPermission functions, adding permission checks to actions or server functions, creating features requiring authorization, or ensuring client-server permission consistency.
- unit-test: Guide for writing unit tests with Vitest. Use when writing tests for service functions, pure logic, or webhook handlers. Covers TDD Red-Green-Refactor cycle, Arrange-Act-Assert pattern, anti-patterns, pure function testing, and mocking at boundaries.
- tanstack-start-boundaries: TanStack Start server/client code boundary patterns. Use when: debugging virtual module resolution errors (tanstack-start-injected-head-scripts, tanstack-start-manifest, #tanstack-router-entry), importing from @tanstack/react-start/server, using getCookie/getRequestHeaders/getRequestUrl, adding providers to router.tsx, or when Vite fails to resolve TanStack Start imports. Covers createServerFn patterns, dynamic imports, and proper placement of server-only code.
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills using TypeScript/Bun scripts (Windows-compatible). Use when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
- drag-and-drop: Implement drag and drop using @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop. Use when implementing sortable lists, reorderable items, kanban boards, or any drag-drop interactions. Covers draggable setup, drop targets, edge detection, drag previews, and critical state management patterns to avoid performance issues.
- my-pr-comments: Loads the pr review comments for the current branch.
- review: Centralizes persona-driven code reviews (Carmack, React core, Anders, React Router, Next.js, etc.) so Claude can pick or combine expert viewpoints when the user asks for a code review or perspective-specific critique. Use when user asks for code review, architecture feedback, or expert perspective on implementation.
- local-db: Handle local database schema changes with Drizzle and PlanetScale. Use when making schema changes to src/db/schema.ts, adding/modifying database tables or columns, or when asked about database migrations. Covers the push-based local development workflow and when to generate migrations.
- e2e-test: End-to-end testing with Playwright. Use when writing E2E tests, implementing page objects, setting up test fixtures, handling authentication in tests, adding visual regression testing, or configuring E2E tests for CI. Covers when to use E2E vs integration tests, critical path identification, page object pattern, test isolation, and CI considerations.
- team-memory: Store, search, and manage team memory from AI coding sessions. Use /remember to save observations (conventions, gotchas, debugging tips, architecture decisions, workflows). Use /recall to search memories by semantic query. Use /feedback to rate memory quality (helpful/harmful/irrelevant). Triggers on any request to save, recall, search, or rate team knowledge.
- alchemy-cloudflare: Alchemy IaC patterns for deploying TanStack Start apps to Cloudflare Workers with PlanetScale databases via Hyperdrive. Use when setting up new TanStack Start projects, configuring Alchemy deployments, working with PlanetScale/Drizzle migrations, local development with Cloudflare bindings, or deploying to custom domains.

- swarm:status: Check swarm coordination status - workers, messages, reservations

input.query:
SELECT ce.id as event_id, ce.track_workout_id, tw.id as tw_id, w.name as workout_name
FROM competition_events ce
LEFT JOIN track_workouts tw ON tw.id = ce.track_workout_id
LEFT JOIN workouts w ON w.id = tw.workout_id
WHERE ce.competition_id = 'comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz'
DESCRIBE event_division_mappings

input.query:
SELECT edm.track_workout_id, edm.division_id, sl.label as division_label, w.name as workout_name
FROM event_division_mappings edm
LEFT JOIN scaling_levels sl ON sl.id = edm.division_id
LEFT JOIN track_workouts tw ON tw.id = edm.track_workout_id
LEFT JOIN workouts w ON w.id = tw.workout_id
WHERE edm.competition_id = 'comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz'
ORDER BY w.name, sl.label

input.query:
SELECT user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT division_id) as num_divisions, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT division_id) as divisions, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT id) as registration_ids
FROM competition_registrations
WHERE event_id = 'comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz' AND status = 'active'
GROUP BY user_id
HAVING num_divisions > 1
ORDER BY num_divisions DESC

input.query:
SELECT
  cr.user_id,
  u.email,
  u.first_name,
  u.last_name,
  COUNT(DISTINCT cr.division_id) as num_divisions,
  GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT sl.label ORDER BY sl.label SEPARATOR ' | ') as divisions
FROM competition_registrations cr
LEFT JOIN scaling_levels sl ON sl.id = cr.division_id
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = cr.user_id
WHERE cr.event_id = 'comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz' AND cr.status = 'active'
GROUP BY cr.user_id, u.email, u.first_name, u.last_name
HAVING num_divisions > 1
ORDER BY num_divisions DESC, u.last_name
DESCRIBE scores

input.query:
SELECT
  s.user_id,
  u.first_name,
  u.last_name,
  u.email,
  s.competition_event_id,
  w.name as workout_name,
  s.scaling_level_id,
  sl.label as score_division,
  s.score_value,
  s.recorded_at,
  s.status
FROM scores s
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = s.user_id
LEFT JOIN competition_events ce ON ce.id = s.competition_event_id
LEFT JOIN track_workouts tw ON tw.id = ce.track_workout_id
LEFT JOIN workouts w ON w.id = tw.workout_id
LEFT JOIN scaling_levels sl ON sl.id = s.scaling_level_id

input.query:
SELECT
  u.last_name, u.first_name, u.email,
  COUNT(*) as score_count,
  GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT CONCAT(IFNULL(w.name, s.competition_event_id), ' => ', IFNULL(sl.label,'?')) ORDER BY w.name SEPARATOR ' || ') as scores
FROM scores s
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = s.user_id
LEFT JOIN track_workouts tw ON tw.id = s.competition_event_id
LEFT JOIN workouts w ON w.id = tw.workout_id
LEFT JOIN scaling_levels sl ON sl.id = s.scaling_level_id
WHERE s.competition_event_id IN (
  'trwk_01KNS2EXXJK1ZEEWZJSMTMTWVR','trwk_01KNS2GBP1DS8SV1VAGFB7XXHZ','trwk_01KNS2GYD5PXSYV1X5YKTNJNG4',
  'trwk_01KNS3EQ47TYXWEZ1HRSP45N4S','trwk_01KNS3TR6XXGW7FESEN6815E5G','REDACTED',
  'REDACTED','trwk_01KNW66SYJE604J4BJVPQ35RVC','trwk_01KNV3K6KFENDYBM0XD9VQ5KAJ',
  'trwk_01KNVZYB6R1CZ6YFFWB4ATP0AH'
)
AND s.user_id IN (
  'usr_s2f4j6zwth50p2shil0n1dpp','usr_qap5rodzau2bbbev1j7400b9','usr_01KKG6PHZ5DHRX6YPW7KQMBC54',

content.text: I have enough data to write the findings. Let me also confirm the specific collision count.

input.query:
SELECT
  cr.user_id,
  u.first_name, u.last_name,
  cr.division_id,
  sl.label as registered_division
FROM competition_registrations cr
LEFT JOIN scaling_levels sl ON sl.id = cr.division_id
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = cr.user_id
WHERE cr.event_id = 'comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz'
  AND cr.status = 'active'
  AND cr.user_id IN (
    SELECT user_id FROM competition_registrations
    WHERE event_id = 'comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz' AND status='active'
    GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT division_id) > 1
  )
ORDER BY u.last_name, u.first_name, sl.label

input.content:
# MWFC Online Qualifier 2026 — Score Overwrite Audit

Production data audit of the `(competitionEventId, userId)` unique-index bug for competition
`mwfc-mountain-west-fitness-championship-online-qualifier-2026` (`comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz`).

Branch queried: `wodsmith-db.main` (PlanetScale, org `wodsmith`). Pulled 2026-04-19.

## Bug summary

`scores_table` has a unique index on `(competition_event_id, user_id)`. In `scores` the
`competition_event_id` column actually stores the **`track_workout_id`**, so any user who is
registered in multiple divisions that share the same track workout can only ever hold ONE
row for that workout. Each submission uses `ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE`, so the most recent
submission overwrites the previous division's `scaling_level_id` and the row is now
attributed to whichever division was submitted last.

## Event → division exposure

Each cell is a `track_workout_id` and the set of divisions that list it.

| Workout | track_workout_id | Exposure | # divisions |
|---|---|---|---|
| MWFC OQ Workout #1 | `trwk_01KNS2EXXJK1ZEEWZJSMTMTWVR` | Individual-only | 10 |
| MWFC OQ Workout #2 | `trwk_01KNS3EQ47TYXWEZ1HRSP45N4S` | Individual-only | 10 |
| Part A (indiv) | `trwk_01KNS2GBP1DS8SV1VAGFB7XXHZ` | Individual-only | 10 |
| Part B (indiv) | `trwk_01KNS2GYD5PXSYV1X5YKTNJNG4` | Individual-only | 10 |
| **MWFC OQ Workout #3** | `trwk_01KNS3TR6XXGW7FESEN6815E5G` | **SHARED indiv + team** | 22 |
| **MWFC OQ Workout #4** | `REDACTED` | **SHARED indiv + team** | 22 |
| TEAM MWFC OQ WORKOUT #1 | `REDACTED` | Team-only | 12 |
| Part A (team) | `trwk_01KNW66SYJE604J4BJVPQ35RVC` | Team-only | 12 |
| Part B (team) | `trwk_01KNV3K6KFENDYBM0XD9VQ5KAJ` | Team-only | 12 |
| TEAM MWFC OQ Workout #2 | `trwk_01KNVZYB6R1CZ6YFFWB4ATP0AH` | Team-only | 12 |

### Collision topology

The bug fires whenever a user submits scores across multiple registered divisions that map to
the same `track_workout_id`.

- **Indiv + Team registration** — workouts #3 and #4 collide (the only shared tracks).
- **Indiv RX + Indiv INT** (or any two indiv divisions) — **every individual workout** (#1, #2,
  #3, #4, Part A, Part B indiv) collides because all individual divisions share the same
  `track_workout_id` for each event.
- **Team RX + Team INT** (or any two team divisions) — every team workout collides.
- **Masters Indiv + Open Indiv same gender/scaling** (e.g. Masters Indiv INT + Open Indiv INT) —
  data-wise this is still a collision because the two divisions have distinct
  `scaling_level_id`s, so only one attribution survives per workout row.

## Affected users in this competition

- **Total active registrations**: 293 across 260 unique users.
- **Users with >1 division**: **32**.
- **Of those, 26 have already submitted ≥1 score** on an MWFC track workout (26 confirmed
  data bugs; the remaining 6 have no scores submitted yet).

### Case legend

- **A — Indiv + Team**: collisions on W#3 and W#4 only. Other scores land cleanly in their
  home workouts (individual workouts write to indiv-only tracks, team workouts to team-only
  tracks).
- **B — Indiv + Indiv** (different scaling): collisions on every individual track (W#1, W#2,
  W#3, W#4, Part A, Part B).
- **C — Team + Team** (different scaling): collisions on every team track (team W#1, team
  W#2, team Part A, team Part B, W#3, W#4).
- **D — Indiv + Indiv, same logical scaling but different `scaling_level_id`**: e.g. Masters
  INT + Open INT. Collisions on every individual track, but the athlete arguably wants the
  same score shown in both divisions → under a fix this requires duplicating the row into
  the missing `scaling_level_id` (or mapping at the read layer).
- **E — Team + Team, same logical scaling but different `scaling_level_id`**: like D but
  team-side.

### Full affected-user table

| 2 | Audrey Barlow (`usr_qap5rodzau2bbbev1j7400b9`) | zapzooey.ab@gmail.com | Women's Indiv INT, Women's Team (F/F) INT | A | 0 (no scores yet) | Future W#3/W#4 submissions will collide. |
| 3 | Aaron Barnes (`usr_01KKG6PHZ5DHRX6YPW7KQMBC54`) | aaron.barnes9191@gmail.com | Masters Men Team RX, Men Team (M/M) RX | C | 4 | Every team-track score stored as Masters RX; Men's Team (M/M) RX has zero scores. |
| 4 | Nicholas Blewett (`usr_01KHN24V27XWMEMF96A2FZ309A`) | nblewett63@live.com | Masters Men Indiv INT, Men Indiv INT | D | 5 | All scores stored as Men's Indiv INT; Masters INT division has zero rows. |
| 5 | Gwen Butcher (`usr_01KMKMEGS35VDYXR1HVD7TTK2R`) | gwensolum@gmail.com | Masters Women Indiv INT, Masters Women Team INT | A | 7 | W#2 indiv-only → Masters Indiv. W#3/W#4 shared → stored as Masters Team. Masters Indiv shows no W#3/W#4. |

competition.

### Workout #3 — `trwk_01KNS3TR6XXGW7FESEN6815E5G`

## Breakdown by case

- **Case A (Indiv + Team)**: 20 users — Barlow, Butcher, Coffelt, Dahlman, Hildebrandt,

- **Case B (Indiv + Indiv, different level)**: 4 users — Zac Jones, A. Perttula, M. Ramos,
  D. Thomas. Zac additionally has a Team registration (A overlap).
- **Case C (Team + Team, different level)**: 1 user — Aaron Barnes.
- **Case D (Indiv same scaling, different masters/open `scaling_level_id`)**: 4 users —

  be duplicated across divisions under the fix.
- **Case E (Team same scaling, different masters/open `scaling_level_id`)**: 2 users —
  Gaffaney, Reynolds.

scaling_level_id)`):

1. For each user above, inspect which divisions they are registered in and which
   `scaling_level_id`s currently have score rows.
2. For any shared track (W#3/W#4 indiv+team, or intra-indiv/intra-team splits) where only

   scaling_level_id so both divisions are represented.
3. For Case-D/E users where the intent is identical scores across divisions (e.g. Masters
   + Open same level): always duplicate.
4. For Case-B/C users who actually posted different scores under different divisions

   with the athlete** which score belongs to which division before duplicating. These rows
   indicate the athlete submitted intentionally different values across their two divisions
   and simply lost data to the overwrite.
5. Users with no scores yet (Barlow, Coffelt, Ramos, Richardson, Stulken, D. Thomas) — no
   backfill needed; the fix prevents future collisions.

## Queries used

# MWFC Online Qualifier 2026 — Score Overwrite Audit

Production data audit of the `(competitionEventId, userId)` unique-index bug for competition
`mwfc-mountain-west-fitness-championship-online-qualifier-2026` (`comp_jzelryr8d9p5uubg8wd5iobz`).

Branch queried: `wodsmith-db.main` (PlanetScale, org `wodsmith`). Pulled 2026-04-19.

## Bug summary

`scores_table` has a unique index on `(competition_event_id, user_id)`. In `scores` the
`competition_event_id` column actually stores the **`track_workout_id`**, so any user who is
registered in multiple divisions that share the same track workout can only ever hold ONE
row for that workout. Each submission uses `ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE`, so the most recent
submission overwrites the previous division's `scaling_level_id` and the row is now
attributed to whichever division was submitted last.

## Event → division exposure

Each cell is a `track_workout_id` and the set of divisions that list it.

| Workout | track_workout_id | Exposure | # divisions |
|---|---|---|---|
| MWFC OQ Workout #1 | `trwk_01KNS2EXXJK1ZEEWZJSMTMTWVR` | Individual-only | 10 |
| MWFC OQ Workout #2 | `trwk_01KNS3EQ47TYXWEZ1HRSP45N4S` | Individual-only | 10 |
| Part A (indiv) | `trwk_01KNS2GBP1DS8SV1VAGFB7XXHZ` | Individual-only | 10 |
| Part B (indiv) | `trwk_01KNS2GYD5PXSYV1X5YKTNJNG4` | Individual-only | 10 |
| **MWFC OQ Workout #3** | `trwk_01KNS3TR6XXGW7FESEN6815E5G` | **SHARED indiv + team** | 22 |
| **MWFC OQ Workout #4** | `REDACTED` | **SHARED indiv + team** | 22 |
| TEAM MWFC OQ WORKOUT #1 | `REDACTED` | Team-only | 12 |
| Part A (team) | `trwk_01KNW66SYJE604J4BJVPQ35RVC` | Team-only | 12 |
| Part B (team) | `trwk_01KNV3K6KFENDYBM0XD9VQ5KAJ` | Team-only | 12 |
| TEAM MWFC OQ Workout #2 | `trwk_01KNVZYB6R1CZ6YFFWB4ATP0AH` | Team-only | 12 |

### Collision topology

The bug fires whenever a user submits scores across multiple registered divisions that map to
the same `track_workout_id`.

- **Indiv + Team registration** — workouts #3 and #4 collide (the only shared tracks).
- **Indiv RX + Indiv INT** (or any two indiv divisions) — **every individual workout** (#1, #2,
  #3, #4, Part A, Part B indiv) collides because all individual divisions share the same
  `track_workout_id` for each event.
- **Team RX + Team INT** (or any two team divisions) — every team workout collides.
- **Masters Indiv + Open Indiv same gender/scaling** (e.g. Masters Indiv INT + Open Indiv INT) —
  data-wise this is still a collision because the two divisions have distinct
  `scaling_level_id`s, so only one attribution survives per workout row.

## Affected users in this competition

- **Total active registrations**: 293 across 260 unique users.
- **Users with >1 division**: **32**.
- **Of those, 26 have already submitted ≥1 score** on an MWFC track workout (26 confirmed
  data bugs; the remaining 6 have no scores submitted yet).

### Case legend

- **A — Indiv + Team**: collisions on W#3 and W#4 only. Other scores land cleanly in their
  home workouts (individual workouts write to indiv-only tracks, team workouts to team-only
  tracks).
- **B — Indiv + Indiv** (different scaling): collisions on every individual track (W#1, W#2,
  W#3, W#4, Part A, Part B).
- **C — Team + Team** (different scaling): collisions on every team track (team W#1, team
  W#2, team Part A, team Part B, W#3, W#4).
- **D — Indiv + Indiv, same logical scaling but different `scaling_level_id`**: e.g. Masters
  INT + Open INT. Collisions on every individual track, but the athlete arguably wants the
  same score shown in both divisions → under a fix this requires duplicating the row into
  the missing `scaling_level_id` (or mapping at the read layer).
- **E — Team + Team, same logical scaling but different `scaling_level_id`**: like D but
  team-side.

### Full affected-user table

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Zac Jones (`usr_s2f4j6zwth50p2shil0n1dpp`) | zacjones93@gmail.com | Men's Indiv INT, Men's Indiv RX, Men's Team (M/M) INT | **B + A** | 5 | All indiv scores stored as INT; RX row has no indiv scores. W#3/W#4 not yet submitted. |
| 2 | Audrey Barlow (`usr_qap5rodzau2bbbev1j7400b9`) | zapzooey.ab@gmail.com | Women's Indiv INT, Women's Team (F/F) INT | A | 0 (no scores yet) | Future W#3/W#4 submissions will collide. |
| 3 | Aaron Barnes (`usr_01KKG6PHZ5DHRX6YPW7KQMBC54`) | aaron.barnes9191@gmail.com | Masters Men Team RX, Men Team (M/M) RX | C | 4 | Every team-track score stored as Masters RX; Men's Team (M/M) RX has zero scores. |
| 4 | Nicholas Blewett (`usr_01KHN24V27XWMEMF96A2FZ309A`) | nblewett63@live.com | Masters Men Indiv INT, Men Indiv INT | D | 5 | All scores stored as Men's Indiv INT; Masters INT division has zero rows. |
| 5 | Gwen Butcher (`usr_01KMKMEGS35VDYXR1HVD7TTK2R`) | gwensolum@gmail.com | Masters Women Indiv INT, Masters Women Team INT | A | 7 | W#2 indiv-only → Masters Indiv. W#3/W#4 shared → stored as Masters Team. Masters Indiv shows no W#3/W#4. |

content.text:
Findings written to `docs/bug-reports/mwfc-score-overwrite-audit.md`.

**Headline numbers for MWFC OQ 2026:**
- 293 active registrations / 260 unique users
- **32 users registered in multiple divisions** (affected scope)
- **26 of those already have submitted scores** that are (or will become) corrupted by the unique-index collision; 6 have no scores yet

**Collision topology confirmed**:
- Shared indiv+team: only `MWFC OQ Workout #3` and `#4`
- Intra-individual (2+ indiv divisions): ALL indiv tracks collide (#1, #2, Part A, Part B, #3, #4)
- Intra-team: ALL team tracks collide
- Masters+Open same scaling still collides because they have distinct `scaling_level_id`s

**Notable tricky cases** (scores split across divisions, intentional or not — confirm with athlete before backfill): Jimmy Garrison, Nicholas Johnson, McKenzie Kravetsky, Jordan Nickel, Karisma Rapp, Samantha Reynolds.

**Important implementation detail surfaced**: `scores.competition_event_id` actually stores a `track_workout_id`, not a `competition_events.id`. Factor that into the index-fix migration.

#!/usr/bin/env tsx
/**
 * Modular seed script for PlanetScale (MySQL).
 *
 * Usage:
 *   DATABASE_URL=mysql://... pnpm tsx scripts/seed/index.ts [--skip-cleanup] [--only <seeder>]
 *
 * Options:
 *   --skip-cleanup    Skip deleting existing data before seeding
 *   --only <name>     Run only a specific seeder (e.g. "03-users")
 */

import { createClient, closeClient } from "./db"
import { cleanup } from "./cleanup"

// Import all seeders in order
import { seed as seedGlobalDefaults } from "./seeders/01-global-defaults"
import { seed as seedBilling } from "./seeders/02-billing"
import { seed as seedUsers } from "./seeders/03-users"
import { seed as seedTeams } from "./seeders/04-teams"
import { seed as seedTeamMemberships } from "./seeders/05-team-memberships"
import { seed as seedTeamEntitlements } from "./seeders/06-team-entitlements"
import { seed as seedScheduling } from "./seeders/07-scheduling"
import { seed as seedMovementsTags } from "./seeders/08-movements-tags"
import { seed as seedWorkouts } from "./seeders/09-workouts"
import { seed as seedProgramming } from "./seeders/10-programming"
import { seed as seedCompetition } from "./seeders/11-competition"
import { seed as seedResults } from "./seeders/12-results"
import { seed as seedOrganizer } from "./seeders/13-organizer"
import { seed as seedSeriesLeaderboard } from "./seeders/14-series-leaderboard"
import { seed as seedVideoSubmissions } from "./seeders/14-video-submissions"
import { seed as seedSubEvents } from "./seeders/15-sub-events"
import { seed as seedWinterThrowdownScores } from "./seeders/16-winter-throwdown-scores"
import { seed as seedSeriesEventTemplates } from "./seeders/17-series-event-templates"
import { seed as seedRegistrationQuestions } from "./seeders/18-registration-questions"
import { seed as seedBroadcasts } from "./seeders/19-broadcasts"

const seeders = [
	{ name: "01-global-defaults", fn: seedGlobalDefaults },
	{ name: "02-billing", fn: seedBilling },
	{ name: "03-users", fn: seedUsers },
	{ name: "04-teams", fn: seedTeams },
	{ name: "05-team-memberships", fn: seedTeamMemberships },
	{ name: "06-team-entitlements", fn: seedTeamEntitlements },
	{ name: "07-scheduling", fn: seedScheduling },
	{ name: "08-movements-tags", fn: seedMovementsTags },
	{ name: "09-workouts", fn: seedWorkouts },
	{ name: "10-programming", fn: seedProgramming },
	{ name: "11-competition", fn: seedCompetition },
	{ name: "12-results", fn: seedResults },
	{ name: "13-organizer", fn: seedOrganizer },
	{ name: "14-series-leaderboard", fn: seedSeriesLeaderboard },
	{ name: "14-video-submissions", fn: seedVideoSubmissions },
	{ name: "15-sub-events", fn: seedSubEvents },
	{ name: "16-winter-throwdown-scores", fn: seedWinterThrowdownScores },
	{ name: "17-series-event-templates", fn: seedSeriesEventTemplates },
	{ name: "18-registration-questions", fn: seedRegistrationQuestions },
	{ name: "19-broadcasts", fn: seedBroadcasts },
]

async function main() {
	const args = process.argv.slice(2)
	const skipCleanup = args.includes("--skip-cleanup")
	const onlyIdx = args.indexOf("--only")
	const onlyName = onlyIdx !== -1 ? args[onlyIdx + 1] : null

	if (onlyName) {
		const found = seeders.find((s) => s.name === onlyName)
		if (!found) {
			console.error(
				`Seeder "${onlyName}" not found. Available: ${seeders.map((s) => s.name).join(", ")}`,
			)
			process.exit(1)
		}
	}

	const client = await createClient()
	console.log("Connected to database\n")

	if (!skipCleanup) {
		await cleanup(client)
	}

	const seedersToRun = onlyName
		? seeders.filter((s) => s.name === onlyName)
		: seeders

	const startTime = Date.now()

	for (const seeder of seedersToRun) {
		await seeder.fn(client)
	}

	const elapsed = ((Date.now() - startTime) / 1000).toFixed(1)
	console.log(`\nAll seeders completed in ${elapsed}s`)
	await closeClient()
}

main().catch((err) => {
	console.error("Fatal error:", err)
	process.exit(1)
})

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * PR Environment Database Seeding Script
3	 *
4	 * Seeds a remote D1 database with demo data for PR preview environments.
5	 * This script is designed to run in CI (GitHub Actions) to populate
6	 * PR-specific databases with minimal test data.
7	 *
8	 * SAFETY: Only runs when STAGE env var starts with 'pr-' (e.g., 'pr-42')
9	 *
10	 * Usage:
11	 *   STAGE=pr-42 pnpm db:seed:pr
12	 *
13	 * Database naming convention: wodsmith-db-{STAGE}
14	 *   e.g., wodsmith-db-pr-42
15	 *
16	 * Test User Credentials:
17	 *   Email: demo@wodsmith.com
18	 *   Password: DemoPassword123!
19	 */
20	
21	import {execSync} from 'node:child_process'
22	import {existsSync} from 'node:fs'
23	import {dirname, join} from 'node:path'
24	import {fileURLToPath} from 'node:url'
25	
26	const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
27	const SCRIPTS_DIR = __dirname
28	const APP_DIR = join(__dirname, '..')
29	
30	/**
31	 * Gets the STAGE environment variable and validates it's a PR stage.
32	 * @returns The validated stage value
33	 * @throws Error if STAGE is not set or doesn't start with 'pr-'
34	 */
35	function getAndValidateStage(): string {
36	  const stage = process.env.STAGE
37	
38	  if (!stage) {
39	    console.error('❌ STAGE environment variable is required')
40	    console.error('   Usage: STAGE=pr-42 pnpm db:seed:pr')
41	    process.exit(1)
42	  }
43	
44	  // Safety check: only allow PR stages to prevent accidental seeding of prod/staging
45	  if (!stage.startsWith('pr-')) {
46	    console.error(`❌ STAGE must start with 'pr-' for safety (got: ${stage})`)
47	    console.error('   This script is only intended for PR preview environments.')
48	    console.error('   For other environments, use the appropriate seed scripts.')
49	    process.exit(1)
50	  }
51	
52	  return stage
53	}
54	
55	/**
56	 * Constructs the database name from the stage.
57	 * @param stage - The stage value (e.g., 'pr-42')
58	 * @returns The database name (e.g., 'wodsmith-db-pr-42')
59	 */
60	function getDbName(stage: string): string {
61	  return `wodsmith-db-${stage}`
62	}
63	
64	/**
65	 * Runs a shell command with error handling.
66	 * @param command - The command to run
67	 * @param description - A description for logging
68	 */
69	function runCommand(command: string, description: string): void {
70	  console.log(`\n📦 ${description}...`)
71	  try {
72	    execSync(command, {
73	      cwd: APP_DIR,
74	      stdio: 'inherit',
75	    })
76	    console.log(`✅ ${description} completed`)
77	  } catch (error) {
78	    console.error(`❌ ${description} failed`)
79	    throw error
80	  }
81	}
82	
83	/**
84	 * Main entry point for PR database seeding.
85	 */
86	async function main(): Promise<void> {
87	  console.log('🚀 PR Environment Database Seeding')
88	  console.log('='.repeat(50))
89	
90	  // Validate STAGE environment variable
91	  const stage = getAndValidateStage()
92	  const dbName = getDbName(stage)
93	
94	  console.log(`📊 Stage: ${stage}`)
95	  console.log(`📊 Database: ${dbName}`)
96	
97	  // Check that seed file exists
98	  const seedFile = join(SCRIPTS_DIR, 'seed-pr.sql')
99	  if (!existsSync(seedFile)) {
100	    console.error(`❌ Seed file not found: ${seedFile}`)
101	    process.exit(1)
102	  }
103	
104	  console.log(`📄 Seed file: ${seedFile}`)
105	
106	  // Run the seed against the remote database
107	  // Note: This assumes migrations have already been applied by the deploy process
108	  runCommand(
109	    `wrangler d1 execute ${dbName} --remote --file=./scripts/seed-pr.sql`,
110	    `Seeding ${dbName} with PR demo data`,
111	  )
112	
113	  console.log('\n' + '='.repeat(50))
114	  console.log('✅ PR database seeding complete!')
115	  console.log('\nDemo credentials:')
116	  console.log('  Email:    demo@wodsmith.com')
117	  console.log('  Password: DemoPassword123!')
118	}
119	
120	main().catch((error) => {
121	  console.error('\n❌ PR database seeding failed:', error.message)
122	  process.exit(1)
123	})
124

import type { Connection } from "mysql2/promise"
import { batchInsert, now } from "../helpers"

export async function seed(client: Connection): Promise<void> {
	console.log("Seeding global defaults...")

	const ts = now()

	await batchInsert(client, "scaling_groups", [
		{
			id: "sgrp_global_default",
			title: "Standard Scaling",
			description:
				"Default Rx+, Rx, and Scaled levels for backward compatibility",
			team_id: null,
			is_default: 1,
			is_system: 1,
			created_at: ts,
			updated_at: ts,
			update_counter: 0,
		},
	])

	await batchInsert(client, "scaling_levels", [
		{
			id: "slvl_global_rxplus",
			scaling_group_id: "sgrp_global_default",
			label: "Rx+",
			position: 0,
			team_size: 1,
			created_at: ts,
			updated_at: ts,
			update_counter: 0,
		},
		{
			id: "slvl_global_rx",
			scaling_group_id: "sgrp_global_default",
			label: "Rx",
			position: 1,
			team_size: 1,
			created_at: ts,
			updated_at: ts,
			update_counter: 0,
		},
		{
			id: "slvl_global_scaled",
			scaling_group_id: "sgrp_global_default",
			label: "Scaled",
			position: 2,
			team_size: 1,
			created_at: ts,
			updated_at: ts,
			update_counter: 0,
		},
	])
}

import type { Connection } from "mysql2/promise"
import { batchInsert, now } from "../helpers"

export async function seed(client: Connection): Promise<void> {

	const ts = now()

	function mem(

		userId: string,
		roleId: string,
		metadata?: string | null,

			id,
			team_id: teamId,
			user_id: userId,
			role_id: roleId,
			is_system_role: 1,
			joined_at: ts,
			created_at: ts,
			updated_at: ts,
			update_counter: 0,
			is_active: 1,
			metadata: metadata ?? null,
		}
	}

	await batchInsert(client, "team_memberships", [
		// Core team memberships

file.content:
import type { Connection } from "mysql2/promise"

/**
 * Delete all rows from all tables in reverse dependency order.
 * Matches the cleanup section from seed.sql but uses MySQL table names.
 */
export async function cleanup(client: Connection): Promise<void> {
	const tables = [
		// Broadcasts (before competitions)
		"competition_broadcast_recipients",
		"competition_broadcasts",
		// Series tables (before competitions/scaling)
		"series_division_mappings",
		"series_template_divisions",
		// Competition deep children
		"score_rounds",
		"scores",
		"judge_heat_assignments",
		"judge_assignment_versions",
		"competition_judge_rotations",
		"video_submissions",
		"submission_window_notifications",
		"event_judging_sheets",
		"event_resources",
		"competition_events",
		"competition_heat_assignments",
		"competition_heats",
		"volunteer_registration_answers",
		"competition_registration_answers",
		"competition_registration_questions",
		"competition_registrations",
		"competition_venues",
		"competition_divisions",
		"competitions",
		"competition_groups",
		"addresses",
		// Scheduling
		"scheduled_classes",
		"generated_schedules",
		"schedule_template_class_required_skills",
		"schedule_template_classes",
		"schedule_templates",
		// Programming
		"scheduled_workout_instances",
		"team_programming_tracks",
		"track_workouts",
		"programming_tracks",
		// Workouts and related
		"sets",
		"results",
		"workout_scaling_descriptions",
		"workout_movements",
		"workout_tags",
		"workouts",
		"spicy_tags",
		"movements",
		// Scaling
		"scaling_levels",
		"scaling_groups",
		// Coaches / Classes
		"coach_to_skills",
		"class_catalog_to_skills",
		"coach_blackout_dates",
		"coach_recurring_unavailability",
		"coaches",
		"class_catalogs",
		"skills",
		"locations",
		// Sponsors
		"sponsors",
		"sponsor_groups",
		// Commerce
		"purchased_items",
		"credit_transactions",
		"commerce_purchases",
		"commerce_products",
		// Auth
		"passkey_credentials",
		"waiver_signatures",
		"waivers",
		// Team hierarchy (children before parents)
		"affiliates",
		"organizer_requests",
		"team_invitations",
		"team_memberships",
		"team_roles",
		"team_entitlement_overrides",
		"team_feature_entitlements",
		"team_limit_entitlements",
		"team_usages",
		"team_addons",
		"team_subscriptions",
		"entitlements",
		// Plans and features (before teams)
		"plan_limits",
		"plan_features",
		"plans",
		"limits",
		"features",
		"entitlement_types",
		// Core entities
		"teams",
		"users",
	]

	console.log("Cleaning up all tables...")
	for (const table of tables) {
		try {
			await client.execute(`DELETE FROM \`${table}\``)
		} catch {
			// Table might not exist yet, that's OK
		}
	}
	console.log(`  Cleaned ${tables.length} tables\n`)
}

input.query:
SELECT c.slug, c.name, c.status, c.competition_type, t.name as organizing_team, u.email as organizer_owner
FROM competitions c
JOIN teams t ON t.id = c.organizing_team_id
LEFT JOIN team_memberships tm ON tm.team_id = t.id AND tm.role_id = 'owner'
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = tm.user_id
WHERE c.id = 'comp_mwfc_oq'

input.query:
SELECT cr.user_id, u.email, COUNT(DISTINCT cr.division_id) as divs, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT sl.label ORDER BY sl.label SEPARATOR ' | ') as divisions
FROM competition_registrations cr
JOIN users u ON u.id = cr.user_id
JOIN scaling_levels sl ON sl.id = cr.division_id
WHERE cr.event_id = 'comp_mwfc_oq' AND cr.status = 'active'
GROUP BY cr.user_id, u.email
HAVING divs > 1
ORDER BY divs DESC, u.email

content.text:
Seed + scenario replica complete on `score-division-unique` branch.

**Seed**: full `pnpm db:seed` ran against your local pscale proxy (14.4s, all 19 seeders green).

**MWFC replica** (`comp_mwfc_oq`, slug `mwfc-online-qualifier-2026`, type `online`, status `published`):
- Organizing team: `CrossFit Box One` owned by `admin@example.com` (usr_demo1admin)
- 6 divisions (Men Indiv INT/RX, Men Team INT/RX, Women Indiv INT, Women Team INT)
- 6 events / 6 workouts (W#1, W#2 indiv-only · W#3, W#4 **shared indiv+team** · Team W#1, Team W#2 team-only)
- 24 event_division_mappings matching the production exposure pattern
- 15 registrations across 3 collision cases + 6 clean controls

**Collisions staged** (seed athletes reused):
| User | Case | Divisions | Visible collision |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mike@athlete.com` | A (indiv+team) | Men Indiv RX + Men Team RX | W#3 row → Indiv RX only; W#4 row → Team RX only |
| `alex.turner@athlete.com` | B (indiv+indiv) | Men Indiv INT + Men Indiv RX | W#1 INT, W#2/W#3 RX, W#4 INT — neither division complete |
| `marcus.reed@athlete.com` | C (team+team) | Men Team INT + Men Team RX | W#3/TeamW#1 RX, W#4/TeamW#2 INT — neither complete |

**Controls** (no collision): Nathan (Indiv RX), Brandon+Derek (Team RX), Sarah (W Indiv INT), Emma+Megan (W Team INT).

**Current schema state** on branch: unique index still `(competition_event_id, user_id)` — i.e. the bug is live and reproducible; applying the 3-column fix + backfill can now be tested end-to-end.
